subject: Databases
title: Choosing the Right Database
description: A quick win: understand Choosing the Right Database.

hook.text: Postgres, MongoDB, Redis, Neo4j — how do you choose? Start with your data shape and access patterns.

buildup.text: Structured data with relationships? Relational. Flexible documents? Document DB. Pure speed? Key-value.

discovery.text: Most apps start with PostgreSQL. It handles 90% of use cases well. Add specialized DBs as needed.

twist.text: Polyglot persistence uses multiple databases in one app. Each service picks the best DB for its job.

climax.text: Don't over-engineer. One database is fine until you prove otherwise with real data and load.

punchline.text: Start with Postgres. Specialize when the data demands it.

quiz.question: What does polyglot persistence mean?
options: Using multiple database types in one application, each chosen for its strengths
Writing database queries in multiple programming languages
Storing data in multiple geographic regions
